title: Day 20 Quiz — React Performance Optimization
description: Verify memo, hooks, lazy loading, memory concepts, and optimization judgment.

questions.prompt: React.memo prevents re-render when props are shallow-equal to previous render.
questions.explanation: memo bails out if shallow compare finds same props.
useCallback caches the function itself between renders when deps unchanged.

questions.prompt: Primary benefit of React.lazy and code splitting?

options.text: Smaller initial bundle — load routes on demand

questions.explanation: Dynamic import splits chunks loaded when user navigates to route.

questions.prompt: Which cause unnecessary child re-renders with React.memo? Select all that apply.

options.text: New object literal props each render

questions.explanation: New function/object references each render break shallow equality.

questions.prompt: Wrap lazy routes with {{blank1}} and provide a fallback UI.
questions.explanation: Suspense catches loading state while lazy chunk downloads.

questions.prompt: Does memoized child re-render when parent passes inline () => {} each time?
questions.explanation: New function reference every parent render — memo comparison fails.

questions.prompt: When should you NOT use React.memo?

options.text: Cheap component always receiving new props
Large table cell with stable data

questions.explanation: Memo overhead exceeds benefit when props always change or render is trivial.
React.lazy accepts dynamic import returning default export.

questions.prompt: Explain difference between useMemo and useCallback.
questions.sampleAnswer: useMemo caches a computed value result between renders when dependencies unchanged. useCallback caches a function reference itself — useful for stable props to memoized children. Both avoid unnecessary work but cache different things.

questions.prompt: Garbage collection reclaims memory when? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: JS GC is automatic when objects unreachable — no manual free().
